history.content:
<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north, row 06, column D) is lava (L), so moving forward would be fatal. The cell to my right (east, row 07, column E) is a safe floor (.). Turning right is the only safe immediate action to avoid the lava and continue exploration towards the east, where more floor cells are visible.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a safe floor cell. To my left is lava, and to my right is a wall. The only safe direction is forward, and moving forward may reveal the goal. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at D07 facing north. The cell immediately in front is D06, which is a safe floor tile. Moving forward will advance toward the open corridor at the top, allowing further exploration toward the west where the goal might be located. There are no visible threats directly ahead.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07 column D. The cell directly ahead (D06) is a safe floor cell. The goal is not visible, so we need to explore the map. Moving forward is safe and will reveal more of the path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at D07 facing north. The cell forward (D06) is a safe floor. To the left (C07) is lava, and to the right (E07) is a wall. Moving forward is the only safe action. It will allow further exploration and potentially reveal a path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is empty (D06 is '.'), so it is safe to move forward. Moving forward will shift my view upward, revealing more of the unknown area above and the right side columns F and G, which may contain a path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>MOVE_FORWARD</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) is a floor (row 06, column D), which is safe to step on. The cells to my left and right are lava and a wall respectively, so I should move forward to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see that directly in front of me (row 06, column D) is a wall (`#`), so I cannot move forward. To my right (column E) is also a wall, so turning right would not help. To my left (column C) is an empty floor cell (`.`), so turning left would allow me to move into that space. I will turn left to face that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly ahead in column D, six steps north. The cell in front of me (06D) is empty floor, and the entire column D up to row 01 is clear of lava or walls. I can safely move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible at D02, directly ahead in the direction I am facing (north). The cells ahead in column D (D06, D05, D04, D03) are all empty floor cells. There are no walls or lava blocking my path forward. The safest immediate action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is at D3, directly ahead of me. The cells in my path (D6, D5, D4) are all floor, and the adjacent cells are lava but not blocking my forward movement. Moving forward is safe and brings me closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible directly ahead at row 04 column D. The path forward is clear: row 06 D is floor, row 05 D is floor, and row 04 D is the goal. There is no lava or wall blocking my way. I will move forward to get closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (D06) is a floor cell (.) and safe to step on. The goal (G) is at D05, just two steps ahead. There is lava to my left (column C) and a wall to my right (column E), so moving forward is the safest and most direct path to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly in front of me at row 06 column D. I can move forward to reach it safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
